registryKey.description: Enable experimental better handling of recursive cases in type inference

Maximum nesting depth of control-flow type assumptions (AssumptionContext). Beyond it, type inference falls back to the un-narrowed context to avoid StackOverflowError/heap exhaustion on pathological def-use/operator chains.
Enable pyi stubs distributed with numpy (see https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/18565)
Enable pyi tensorflow stubs in Typeshed
Require marking namespace packages explicitly, treat regular directories as implicit source roots
When enabled, activates LiteralString inference for Python string literals
It optimises cache usage for storing calculated types.
It enables the use of a library-level cache for PSI elements from packages.
Enables incremental reparse for statement lists
Minimum file size (chars) below which incremental reparse of Python statement lists is skipped.
Hard cap on the statement list size (chars) for incremental reparse.
Maximum statement list size as a percentage of the containing file for incremental reparse.
Allows narrowing types exhaustively to Never
Infer literal types (e.g. Literal[42]) for integer, boolean and string literal expressions
Enable soft-key caching for TypeEvalContext
Enable weak-key caching for TypeEvalContext
Enable specialized class type representing Python's numeric tower (int -> float -> complex)
Enable Any and Unknown support
Enable CSP engine for type inference
Enable variance in subtype checks
Use a process worker pool for skeleton generation to avoid process creation overhead
Show a structural side-by-side type diff in type-mismatch inspection tooltips (otherwise the plain message tooltip is shown)
